rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи взиваю» – 6 стихир: свята – 3 і прор. – 3. Сл., І нині: свята. На стиховні: стихири свята. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Слава, І нині: свята. Відпуст середній з помин. служби свята і прор.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар свята (2), Слава: прор., І нині: свята. Катизми чергові і сідальний свята. Псалом 50. Канон свята і прор. По 9-ій – світильний прор. Слава, І нині: світильний свята. Стиховня – вся свята. По «Отче наш»: тропар м прор., Слава, І нині: свята.
Часи: тропар свята, також на 3-му і 9-му ще Слава: прор., кондаки – поперемінно, тобто: на 1-му і 6-му – свята, на 3-му і 9-му – прор.
Літургія: Тропар і кондак свята. Прокімен, алилуя, причасний – свята. Ап. – 2 Кор.; 190 зач.; 10, 7-18. Єв. – Мр. 14 зач.; 3, 28-35. Замість «Достойно» – приспів і ірмос 9-ї пісні канону свята.

troparion_en.text: You arrived by your mother's prayers, bearing the name “God has heard.” /  You were called by the Lord from infancy, O revered Prophet Samuel,  /  you shepherded the rebellious people well and became the founder of the kingdom of Israel;  /  entreat the True God for us who honor you with faith /   that He may save our souls.

kontakion_en.text: O great Samuel, who anointed the holy God-seer David,  / hasten to ask that we may be anointed with the peace of the Holy Spirit, / and to be strangers to evil, as David was for Saul's tormented soul.1

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Dormition of the Mother of God

troparion.text: In giving birth you preserved your virginity, / in falling asleep you did not forsake the world, O Theotokos. / You were translated to life, O Mother of Life, / and by your prayers, you deliver our souls from death.

kontakion.text: Neither the tomb, nor death could hold the Theotokos, / who is constant in prayer and our firm hope in her intercessions. / For being the Mother of Life, / she was translated to life by the One who dwelt in her virginal womb.

troparion.text: You arrived by your mother's prayers, bearing the name “God has heard.” /  You were called by the Lord from infancy, O revered Prophet Samuel,  /  you shepherded the rebellious people well and became the founder of the kingdom of Israel;  /  entreat the True God for us who honor you with faith /   that He may save our souls.

kontakion.text: O great Samuel, who anointed the holy God-seer David,  / hasten to ask that we may be anointed with the peace of the Holy Spirit, / and to be strangers to evil, as David was for Saul's tormented soul.1

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Philip, Bishop of Heraclea and with him the Martyrs Severus, Memnon, and 37 Soldiers in Thrace

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Heliodorus and Dosa of Persia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Heliodorus and Dosa, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Your holy martyr Lucius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Lucius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Holy New Martyr Theokhares at Neopolis in Asia Minor

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
Your Martyr Theokhárēs, O Lord,  / by his contest has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God; /  for, possessed of Your strength, /  he laid the tyrants low and shattered the powerless boldness of the demons.  / By his supplications, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the things on high...”)
You shone forth in your splendor as a bright star,  / proclaiming Christ to this temporal world, O Martyr
Theokhárēs, /  vanquishing the snares of false gods, /  you revealed the true light to the faithful,  / and you
always intercede on behalf of us all.
